questions.question: Explain the difference between 0/1 Knapsack and Fractional Knapsack problems.
keyPoints: 0/1: items taken wholly or not at all
Fractional: fractions of items can be taken
0/1 uses Dynamic Programming
Fractional uses Greedy approach
Greedy works for Fractional but not for 0/1

questions.question: Write the recurrence relation for 0/1 Knapsack and explain each term.
keyPoints: K[i-1][w] = max value excluding current item
K[i-1][w-wt[i-1]] + val[i-1] = value including current item
Condition: wt[i-1] <= w
Base case: K[0][w] = 0, K[i][0] = 0

questions.question: Why is the Knapsack problem called NP-hard? Give reason.
keyPoints: No polynomial-time algorithm exists for arbitrary inputs
Time complexity O(n*W) is pseudo-polynomial
W appears as numeric value not log(W) in input size
Becomes impractical for large W values
Exact solution requires checking exponential combinations

questions.question: Describe the Greedy approach for Fractional Knapsack with its time complexity.
keyPoints: Step 1: Calculate value/weight ratio for each item
Step 2: Sort items in descending order of ratio
Step 3: Add items fully until capacity reached
Step 4: Take fractional part of last item if needed
Time Complexity: O(n log n) dominated by sorting

questions.question: List two real-world applications of Knapsack Problem.
keyPoints: Cargo loading in ships/aircraft - maximize cargo value within weight limit
Budget allocation - select projects within budget for maximum return
Resource allocation in computing - memory/process distribution
Inventory management - selecting items within weight/value constraints
Portfolio optimization - investment selection within capital constraint

questions.question: Explain why Greedy approach fails for 0/1 Knapsack with an example.
keyPoints: Greedy chooses highest value/weight ratio items first
Cannot take fractional parts of items
Example: capacity=50, items: (value=60,weight=30,ratio=2), (value=100,weight=49,ratio≈2.04), (value=100,weight=50,ratio=2)
Greedy picks item2 (ratio highest) with weight 49, leaves capacity 1 unused, total=100

Better solution: items 1+3 with total value 160 within capacity 80>50? No. Better: 60+100=160 not possible. Simple: capacity 10, items: (60,6,ratio=10), (100,10,ratio=10). Greedy picks either - value 60 or 100. But items (50,5,ratio=10) and (60,6,ratio=10) together give 110 within capacity 11>10. Greedy fails.

questions.question: What is memoization? How is it applied to 0/1 Knapsack?
keyPoints: Memoization is top-down dynamic programming
Store results of computed subproblems
Avoid recomputation of same states
In Knapsack: recursively compute K[i][w] and store in table
On subsequent calls, return stored value if already computed
Reduces time complexity compared to naive recursion

questions.question: Solve the following 0/1 Knapsack problem using Dynamic Programming: n=4, capacity=5, values=[6,10,12,6], weights=[2,3,4,5]. Show the complete DP table and trace the solution.
keyPoints: Initialize 5x5 table with zeros
Fill for item1(2,6): w=0-5, only w>=2 gets value 6
Fill for item2(3,10): check combinations
Fill for item3(4,12): check combinations
Fill for item4(5,6): check combinations

Final answer: maximum value = 22 (items 1,2,3: 6+10+12=28? No weight 2+3+4=9>5. Items 2,3: 10+12=22 within weight 3+4=7>5. Items 1,2,4: 6+10+6=22 weight 2+3+5=10. Items 1,3: 6+12=18 weight 6. Items 1,2: 6+10=16 weight 5 - YES! Answer = 16 actually. Wait 2+3=5, value=6+10=16. Check item2+3: 3+4=7>5. Item3+4: 4+5=9>5. Item1+2+3: 2+3+4=9>5. So max is 16 with items 1 and 2.

questions.question: Solve the Fractional Knapsack problem for: capacity=15, items with (value,weight): (10,2), (5,3), (15,5), (7,4), (6,3), (18,10). Show step-by-step solution and final maximum value.
keyPoints: Calculate ratios: 5, 1.67, 3, 1.75, 2, 1.8
Sort in descending order by ratio
Take item1 fully: value=10, weight=2, remaining=13
Take item2 fully: value=15, weight=5, remaining=8
Take item3 fully: value=6, weight=3, remaining=5
Take item4 partially: 5/10 × 18 = 9, remaining=0
Total value = 10+15+6+9 = 40
Maximum value = 40

questions.question: Write the pseudocode for bottom-up Dynamic Programming approach to 0/1 Knapsack. Analyze its time and space complexity.
keyPoints: Input: n items, W capacity, arrays val[0..n-1], wt[0..n-1]
Create K[n+1][W+1] initialized to 0
For i from 1 to n:
For w from 1 to W:
If wt[i-1] <= w:
Else:
Return K[n][W]
Time Complexity: O(n×W)
Space Complexity: O(n×W), can be optimized to O(W)
Pseudo-polynomial because depends on numeric value of W
